description.value: Fully managed, serverless NoSQL key-value and document database from AWS, offering single-digit millisecond performance at any scale

license.comment: Commercial, closed-source AWS service (free tier available for limited operations).

first-release.comment: Amazon DynamoDB was launched in January 2012.

latest-version.comment: DynamoDB is a continuously-deployed managed cloud service with no public, user-facing version number. The DynamoDB Local emulator is versioned separately.

actively-maintained.comment: Actively developed AWS service receiving ongoing feature releases (e.g., multi-Region strong consistency for global tables).

database-type.comment: Primary models are key-value and document store; DB-Engines classifies it as both.

query-language.comment: Native access is via a RESTful HTTP API/SDK. DynamoDB also supports PartiQL, a SQL-compatible query language (subset); mapped to the closest 'sql' tag as there is no PartiQL tag in the schema.

acid-compliant.comment: TransactWriteItems/TransactGetItems provide all-or-nothing, serializable-isolation ACID transactions within a single AWS Region. Cross-Region transactions on global tables are not ACID.

schema-model.comment: Schemaless except that primary key (partition/sort key) attributes and their types must be declared at table creation; all other attributes are free-form. Treated as schema-optional rather than fully schema-less.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Automatically partitions and distributes data across nodes for instant scaling; horizontal scaling is intrinsic to the managed service.

replication.comment: Global Tables provide multi-Region, active-active replication (every replica accepts writes), equivalent to multi-primary.

clustering.comment: Distribution and clustering are managed automatically by AWS; no third-party tooling required (the cluster is abstracted from the user).

cloud-offering.comment: DynamoDB is exclusively an official AWS managed cloud service; there is no self-hosted production deployment.

full-text-search.comment: No built-in full-text search; AWS recommends integrating Amazon OpenSearch Service for full-text search use cases.

json-support.comment: Document types (Map and List) map directly to JSON objects/arrays, nestable up to 32 levels; items are natively document-structured and queryable via PartiQL and expressions.

geospatial.comment: No native geospatial data types or queries; geohashing must be implemented at the application level (e.g., the geo library).

time-series.comment: Commonly used for time-series workloads with TTL-based expiry, but offers no specialized/dedicated time-series engine or functions.

stored-procedures.comment: No server-side stored procedures; server-side logic is handled externally via AWS Lambda triggered by DynamoDB Streams, not inside the database engine.

triggers.comment: DynamoDB Streams + AWS Lambda provide trigger-like, event-driven processing on item-level create/update/delete changes.

views.comment: No SQL-style views or materialized views; secondary indexes (GSIs/LSIs) provide alternate access patterns but are not views.

official-gui.comment: Official AWS Management Console for DynamoDB plus the NoSQL Workbench desktop GUI provided by AWS.

cli-tool.comment: Managed via the official AWS Command Line Interface (aws dynamodb commands).

docker-image.comment: AWS publishes amazon/dynamodb-local (the DynamoDB Local emulator) on Docker Hub, but it is a Verified Publisher image, not a Docker Official Image as the attribute strictly defines.

primary-language.comment: Closed-source AWS service; the implementation language is not publicly documented (DynamoDB's internal storage node has been described as Java-based in talks, but this is not officially confirmed).

github-stars.comment: Closed-source proprietary service with no public GitHub repository; per Assessment Guidelines, GitHub Stars is null for closed-source databases.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Could not retrieve the Stack Overflow 'amazon-dynamodb' tag question count from a reliable source during this pass (stackoverflow.com not fetchable).

db-engines-rank.comment: Ranked #18 overall (and #2 among key-value stores) as of 2026-06-09.

learning-curve.comment: Managed setup is easy and PartiQL is SQL-like, but effective use requires single-table design, partition/sort key modeling, and access-pattern-first thinking that differ from relational habits; rated moderate.
